input: user-item interaction graph and item multi-modal features (e.g., images, text)
output: ranked list of items per user
backbone: lightweight encoder (no auxiliary graphs, no negative sampling)
loss: multi-modal objectives: reconstruction of user-item graph + inter- and intra-modality alignment
key_idea: bootstrap latent contrastive views from user/item representations via dropout, eliminating need for auxiliary graphs and negative samples
datasets: three datasets with 20K-200K nodes (not named in abstract)
